heading: Illegal trafficking in Native American human remains and cultural items
label: 1170 Illegal trafficking in Native American human remains and cultural items
notes: References in TextThe Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act, referred to in text, is , , , which is classified principally to chapter 32 (§ 3001 et seq.) of Title 25, Indians. Amendments2022—Subsec. (a). substituted “1 year and 1 day” for “12 months” and “10 years” for “5 years”.Pub. L. 117–258Subsec. (b). , substituted “10 years” for “5 years”.Pub. L. 103–322

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever knowingly sells, purchases, uses for profit, or transports for sale or profit, the human remains of a Native American without the right of possession to those remains as provided in the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act shall be fined in accordance with this title, or imprisoned not more than 1 year and 1 day, or both, and in the case of a second or subsequent violation, be fined in accordance with this title, or imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.

children.label: b
children.text: Whoever knowingly sells, purchases, uses for profit, or transports for sale or profit any Native American cultural items obtained in violation of the Native American Grave Protection and Repatriation Act shall be fined in accordance with this title, imprisoned not more than one year, or both, and in the case of a second or subsequent violation, be fined in accordance with this title, imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.
